commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Slack preview streaming to enable top-level DM draft previews, delete tool-progress bullet drafts on transitions/final cleanup, update Slack docs/tests/changelog, and adjust raw channel-fetch allowlist line numbers.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. A narrative partial can be queued or in flight when a tool event calls `pushPreviewToolProgress()`, and the PR rotates the draft with `forceNewMessage()` before that narrative draft is flushed.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can update the PR branch to drain narrative drafts before rotating to bullet previews and add focused regression coverage.

target.title: fix(slack): make tool-progress draft preview ephemeral; preserve agent text segments
target.author: shadowwalker2014

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: ShadowWalker2014
commands.command: iteration 2 addressed in commit `22e07c08fd`: **[p2] in-flight bullet sends → orphan preview race** you're right — `deletebulletpreview()` was capturing ids and calling `forcenewmessage()` without draining the draft loop's in-flight send. `working…` sends in flight when the helper ran could complete after we'd reset, leaving an orphan preview. fix: `deletebulletpreview()` now awaits `draftstream.flush()` first. `flush()` drains pending text and waits for in-flight sends to settle, so when `channelid`/`messageid` are read next they point to the real slack message. `forcenewmessage` + `chat.delete` then completes the deletion. `clear()` is still off-limits because it calls `discardpending` → `stop`, but `flush` keeps the loop reusable. **[p3] changelog entry** added under `unreleased` / `fixes` (`channels/slack: …`). single-line, no maintainer attribution. **drive-by** `scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs` allowlist had stale line numbers for `extensions/chutes/models.ts` and `extensions/huggingface/models.ts` (off-by-one) after upstream's `52cd76a9e2` (`fix(providers): isolate model discovery test env guards`) shifted those files by an added import. updated `(535, 542, 142)` → `(536, 543, 143)` so `check-additional-boundaries` passes. happy to split this off into its own pr if preferred — flagging here so it's visible. **test status** 89 files / 897 tests pass. `pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch` passes. `oxfmt --check` clean. pushed; ci re-running.
commands.freeform_prompt: iteration 2 addressed in commit `22e07c08fd`: **[P2] In-flight bullet sends → orphan preview race** You're right — `deleteBulletPreview()` was capturing IDs and calling `forceNewMessage()` without draining the draft loop's in-flight send. `Working…` sends in flight when the helper ran could complete after we'd reset, leaving an orphan preview. Fix: `deleteBulletPreview()` now awaits `draftStream.flush()` FIRST. `flush()` drains pending text and waits for in-flight sends to settle, so when `channelId`/`messageId` are read next they point to the real Slack message. `forceNewMessage` + `chat.delete` then completes the deletion. `clear()` is still off-limits because it calls `discardPending` → `stop`, but `flush` keeps the loop reusable. **[P3] CHANGELOG entry** Added under `Unreleased` / `Fixes` (`Channels/Slack: …`). Single-line, no maintainer attribution. **Drive-by** `scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs` allowlist had stale line numbers for `extensions/chutes/models.ts` and `extensions/huggingface/models.ts` (off-by-one) after upstream's `52cd76a9e2` (`fix(providers): isolate model discovery test env guards`) shifted those files by an added import. Updated `(535, 542, 142)` → `(536, 543, 143)` so `check-additional-boundaries` passes. Happy to split this off into its own PR if preferred — flagging here so it's visible. **Test status** 89 files / 897 tests pass. `pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch` passes. `oxfmt --check` clean. Pushed; CI re-running.

commands.author: ShadowWalker2014
commands.command: thanks for the review — both findings addressed in the latest commit: **[p2] `clear()` stopping the draft stream mid-turn** you're right, `draftstream.clear()` calls `discardpending()` which calls `stop()`. replaced `clear()` at all four mid-turn transition sites with a new local helper `deletebulletpreview()` that: 1. captures `channelid` / `messageid` from the draftstream 2. calls `draftstream.forcenewmessage()` to reset cached ids (does **not** stop the loop) 3. awaits `chat.delete` on the captured ids the stream stays usable after deletion, so a later `update()` (narrative text, another tool burst) correctly posts a fresh permanent message. sites updated: - `updatedraftfrompartial` bullets→narrative transition - `ondraftboundary` bullets case - `kind === "final"` + bullets transition - `finally` cleanup **[p3] dm streaming docs** updated `docs/channels/slack.md:652` so the dm section matches the new behaviour: top-level dms do show the live preview now, edited via `chat.update` while tools run and deleted at end of turn. removed the outdated 'use thread replies or `typingreaction`' guidance. **test status:** 89 files / 894 tests pass; `oxfmt --check` clean. commit pushed; ci re-running.
commands.freeform_prompt: thanks for the review — both findings addressed in the latest commit: **[P2] `clear()` stopping the draft stream mid-turn** You're right, `draftStream.clear()` calls `discardPending()` which calls `stop()`. Replaced `clear()` at all four mid-turn transition sites with a new local helper `deleteBulletPreview()` that: 1. Captures `channelId` / `messageId` from the draftStream 2. Calls `draftStream.forceNewMessage()` to reset cached IDs (does **not** stop the loop) 3. Awaits `chat.delete` on the captured IDs The stream stays usable after deletion, so a later `update()` (narrative text, another tool burst) correctly posts a fresh permanent message. Sites updated: - `updateDraftFromPartial` bullets→narrative transition - `onDraftBoundary` bullets case - `kind === "final"` + bullets transition - `finally` cleanup **[P3] DM streaming docs** Updated `docs/channels/slack.md:652` so the DM section matches the new behaviour: top-level DMs do show the live preview now, edited via `chat.update` while tools run and deleted at end of turn. Removed the outdated 'use thread replies or `typingReaction`' guidance. **Test status:** 89 files / 894 tests pass; `oxfmt --check` clean. Commit pushed; CI re-running.
